title: Cli Version Drift Handshake
goal: Host repos cannot trust startup/status output after package updates unless they can tell whether the executed CLI matches repo-owned expectations; this is package-local and directly actionable here.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

active_milestone.scope: Report executable drift separately from payload drift in status/doctor surfaces.

immediate_next_action: Archive this completed #610 slice after closeout.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "cli_compatibility or executable_drift or dry_run_actions"
uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py
uv run ruff check src tests
uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json
uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/contracts scripts/check tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Expose invoked CLI identity, let host repos declare expected CLI posture, and report executable drift separately from managed payload drift.

execution_run.what happened: Added executable compatibility drift findings and remediation to the CLI compatibility payload; status/doctor now mark executable drift as lifecycle warnings, and doctor emits a concrete manual-review action. Fixed a dogfooding bug where read-only doctor summaries counted dry-run module actions as top-level mutations.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace CLI runtime, CLI compatibility schemas, focused CLI tests, and planning closeout
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "cli_compatibility or executable_drift or dry_run_actions"; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py; uv run pytest tests -q; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/contracts scripts/check tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
execution_run.result for continuation: no #610 continuation remains; non-blocking large-file hotspot dogfooding signal routed to an external GitHub issue without ingesting it
execution_run.next step: Archive this completed plan and close #610 after final closeout checks.

durable_residue.learned constraint: Executable compatibility drift and module payload drift must stay separate because a stale executable can still validate its own known payload.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Agents need doctor/status to say whether the invoked executable itself is trusted before relying on lifecycle output.
durable_residue.canonical owner now: src/agentic_workspace/cli.py compatibility payload, schemas, and tests
durable_residue.promotion trigger: Promote to docs only if host-facing CLI compatibility guidance needs a prose entry.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded #610 slice is implemented and validated; no larger #607 continuation remains in this active lane.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Focused tests, full root tests, ruff, contract tooling checks, structured inventory check, root CLI authority default, and planning doctor all passed.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen if status/doctor again hide executable drift inside payload drift, omit concrete doctor remediation, or report dry-run module actions as applied mutations.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
